summary:
T-lymphocytes are crucial for producing lymphokines and driving delayed hypersensitivity; these functions distinguish them from other components of the immune response such as antibodies which handle immediate hypersensitivity. The core function supporting option \(\boxed{\text{B}}\) involves both aspects but emphasizes cell-mediated immunity over humoral mechanisms. Agent2 concludes based solely on those specific immunological activities attributed uniquely to T-cell biology within human physiology contexts presented here. To reiterate succinctly:

Key point summary: 
\[ \text{T-lymphocytes mainly generate lymphokines and induce delayed hypersensitivity, fitting } \boxed{\text{Option B}}.\] Summary:  
T-lyymphocytes chiefly produce lymphokines and trigger delayed hypersensitivity reactions, highlighting their critical role in cell-mediated immunity distinct from antibody-based defenses seen in immediate hypersensitivity. This supports selecting \( \boxed{\text{B}} \).
